content:
# Qusaiba Formation

**Summary**: A sedimentary formation in the Rub' Al-Khali Basin known for its potential as an unconventional shale play.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] studied the Qusaiba Shale to characterize its brittleness and frackability.

- [[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024]] analyzed the Qusaiba formation at a depth of 3540 m.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] marine/lacustrine (implied by age and lithology)

- [[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024]] analyzed the Qusaiba formation at a depth of 3540 m.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History



## Key Findings Across Sources

- The samples were identified as black, heavy rocks with low porosity (1.65% - 9.44%). ([[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016, p. 46]])

- The maximum offset to avoid phase change for Qusaiba was determined to be 7,080 m (double its depth). ([[2024-felemban|Felemban, 2024, p. 61]])
